Explanation:
The table’s “total” row shows yearly totals: 1939/40 = 504,000; 1940/41 = 352,000; 1941/42 = 407,000; 1942/43 = 541,000; 1943/44 = 681,000; 1944/45 = 270,000. While 1944/45 appears below 350,000, that year has multiple categories blank (e.g., murdered, deaths in prisons & camps, deaths outside of prisons & camps), indicating the figure is not fully comparable to the other years’ complete totals. Considering only fully reported totals, none of the years fall below 350,000 (1940/41 is 352,000, which is above). Therefore, there are no definitive years with total losses below 350,000, aligning with the Prediction Answer “null” to indicate no applicable years.
